summary: B1 confirmed end-to-end: bash WASM missing external_scanner_reset export; allowUndefined:true at tree-sitter.js:1429 masks the load-time check; proxy stub at :1163-1180 throws on first parse-time call. B2 size-driven REJECTED by counterexample (20-line vitest config OOB-crashes; 597-line cli.ts is clean). B2 sub-hypotheses surviving: parser-instance reuse, byte-sequence trigger, build defect.

title: B1 CONFIRMED: bash WASM missing external_scanner_reset; allowUndefined masks the check
recommendation: B1 root cause established. Strings dump shows bash exports {create, destroy, scan, serialize, deserialize} but NOT reset; ts/js export reset. resolveSymbol at tree-sitter.js:1123-1135 returns undefined; allowUndefined:true at :1429 skips reportUndefinedSymbols(); proxy stub at :1163-1180 throws TypeError on first parse-time call. Remediation: skip-list bash files, or rebuild tree-sitter-bash WASM with --link-name reset, or upstream patch web-tree-sitter to honor allowUndefined consistently.

title: B2 size-driven hypothesis REJECTED — 20-line config OOB-crashes
recommendation: vitest.phase-k.config.ts (20 lines / 634 bytes) reproducibly OOB-crashes the typescript WASM while cli.ts (597 lines / 23 389 bytes) parses cleanly. WASM linear-memory-exhaustion-on-large-inputs is NOT the B2 mechanism. Drop size-based skip-list filtering; pursue parser-instance-reuse hypothesis.

title: B2 cross-grammar profile rules out a bash-grammar-only mechanism
recommendation: OOB class crosstab: 33 sh + 10 ts + 6 py + 2 js = 51 files. Grammar-agnostic incidence + lack of size correlation point to a runtime/integration defect rather than a per-grammar build issue. Top remaining sub-hypothesis: shared parserInstance at tree-sitter-parser.ts:712-714 leaks linear-memory state across setLanguage() switches.

title: B1 fault is silent at load due to allowUndefined:true
recommendation: loadLanguage() succeeds because reportUndefinedSymbols() is bypassed when allowUndefined is true. The missing reset symbol therefore surfaces only on first parse-time invocation, distributed across ALL bash files (70 events). Mitigation hook: wrap loadLanguage() to call reportUndefinedSymbols() ourselves, OR run a synthetic parse on grammar load to fast-fail before the file walk.

title: B1 cohort spans tiny-to-huge .sh sizes — single-symbol fault explanation holds
recommendation: Sampled five B1 fixtures: 80, 126, 222, 233, 857 lines. No size correlation. Reinforces F-2.1 — the missing-symbol fault is content-independent and 100% reproducible per-bash-file. Skip-list eligibility for B1 should be by-extension (.sh) or by-grammar (bash), not by-content.

title: Upstream version check deferred — captured as iter 3 backlog
recommendation: WebFetch deferred to keep iter 2 inside 12-call budget. tree-sitter-wasms@0.1.13 build mtime is 2026-03-31; any post-2026-04 upstream bash-reset fix would not be in this install. Iter 3 must reclaim: web-tree-sitter latest, tree-sitter-wasms latest, tree-sitter/issues + Gregoor/tree-sitter-wasms/issues.
